data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-1c3a78f2">
    <title>agent: make missing bead-lifecycle hook diagnostics actionable</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
When the post-attempt triage hook cannot find the nested `bead-lifecycle` skill, the operator sees `post-attempt triage error (<bead> execution_failed): triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)`. The message is technically true but does not say which project root was inspected, which paths were checked, or what command remediates the install.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:90-92` returns `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle` when `hasBeadLifecycleSkill(projectRoot)` is false. `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:218-227` hard-codes the checked paths but returns only a boolean, so callers cannot include the checked path list in diagnostics. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:175-179` logs the hook error verbatim and appends `(continuing)`, which confirms fail-open behavior but hides the actionable remediation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the boolean-only missing-skill path with a diagnostic helper that returns whether the lifecycle skill exists, the resolved project root, and the exact checked paths.
- Update post-attempt triage errors to say the hook was skipped/degraded because `bead-lifecycle` was missing under the project root.
- Include the checked paths `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` in the error text.
- Include remediation in the hook-time error: run `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
- Preserve fail-open behavior: missing lifecycle skill must not change the underlying attempt status or close/park behavior by itself.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not run full `ddx doctor` from inside the hook.
- Do not dispatch the lifecycle hook through a fallback global skill path.
- Do not change lint/readiness/triage JSON output contracts.
- Do not change retry or cooldown policy in this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestPostAttemptTriageHook_MissingSkillErrorIncludesProjectRootPathsAndRemediation` verifies the missing-skill error includes the resolved project root, both checked nested skill paths, `ddx update --force`, and `ddx doctor`.
2. `TestRunPostAttemptTriage_MissingSkillStillFailOpen` verifies a missing lifecycle skill logs the improved diagnostic but returns the original `ExecuteBeadReport` unchanged.
3. `TestHasBeadLifecycleSkillDiagnostics_FindsAgentsOrClaudePath` verifies the diagnostic helper reports success when either `.agents/.../bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` or `.claude/.../bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` exists.
4. `TestPreDispatchLintHook_MissingSkillUsesSameDiagnostic` verifies lint/readiness missing-skill errors use the same checked-path/remediation wording or shared error type.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(PostAttemptTriageHook_MissingSkill|RunPostAttemptTriage_MissingSkill|HasBeadLifecycleSkillDiagnostics|PreDispatchLintHook_MissingSkill)'` green.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:skills, area:cli, kind:bug, reliability, bead-quality, operator-ux, spec:FEAT-010, spec:FEAT-015</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a9d130d0" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="19f88a84d5a106627bc0bf4c4cee6f33fb0bf453" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
I couldn’t finish the bead because the required full gate still fails in this worktree.

What passed:
- The focused diagnostics tests passed.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.

What blocked completion:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` failed on unrelated existing tests:
  - `agent_test.go` still exercises deprecated config values like `gpt-4o` and `turbo`
  - `TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA` times out after 10m in the existing cancellation/probe path

I recorded that blocker here:
- [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1c3a78f2-20260506T225341-aec553c3/.ddx/executions/20260506T225341-aec553c3/no_changes_rationale.txt)

No commit was made.
